# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImageProcessingController < ApplicationController
  before_action :validate_params, only: [:convert]

  ALLOWED_FORMATS = %w[png jpg jpeg gif bmp tiff webp].freeze
  MAX_DIMENSION = 4096
  SIZE_PATTERN = /\A\d{1,4}x\d{1,4}\z/

  def convert
    uploaded_file = params[:image]
    format = params[:format].to_s.downcase.strip
    size = params[:size].to_s.strip

    unless uploaded_file.is_a?(ActionDispatch::Http::UploadedFile)
      return render json: { error: "A valid image file must be uploaded" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    unless ALLOWED_FORMATS.include?(format)
      return render json: { error: "Invalid format. Allowed formats: #{ALLOWED_FORMATS.join(', ')}" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    unless size.match?(SIZE_PATTERN)
      return render json: { error: "Invalid size. Must be in format WIDTHxHEIGHT (e.g., 800x600)" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    width, height = size.split("x").map(&:to_i)
    if width <= 0 || height <= 0 || width > MAX_DIMENSION || height > MAX_DIMENSION
      return render json: { error: "Dimensions must be between 1 and #{MAX_DIMENSION}" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    sanitized_size = "#{width}x#{height}"

    # Validate the uploaded file is actually an image using file command
    unless valid_image?(uploaded_file.tempfile.path)
      return render json: { error: "Uploaded file is not a valid image" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end

    input_path = uploaded_file.tempfile.path
    output_dir = Rails.root.join("tmp", "converted_images")
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(output_dir)

    output_filename = "#{SecureRandom.uuid}.#{format}"
    output_path = File.join(output_dir, output_filename)

    # Use array form of system() to prevent command injection
    # Each argument is passed separately, so shell metacharacters in params
    # cannot be interpreted as shell commands
    convert_path = find_convert_binary

    unless convert_path
      return render json: { error: "ImageMagick convert binary not found" }, status: :internal_server_error
    end

    success = system(
      convert_path,
      input_path,
      "-resize", sanitized_size,
      "-strip",
      "-limit", "memory", "256MiB",
      "-limit", "disk", "512MiB",
      "#{format}:#{output_path}"
    )

    if success && File.exist?(output_path)
      send_file output_path,
                filename: "converted_image.#{format}",
                type: Mime::Type.lookup_by_extension(format)&.to_s || "application/octet-stream",
                disposition: "attachment"
    else
      render json: { error: "Image conversion failed" }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  ensure
    # Clean up output file after sending (deferred)
    if output_path && File.exist?(output_path.to_s)
      Thread.new do
        sleep 5
        FileUtils.rm_f(output_path)
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def validate_params
    unless params[:image].present? && params[:format].present? && params[:size].present?
      render json: { error: "Missing required parameters: image, format, and size" }, status: :bad_request
    end
  end

  def valid_image?(file_path)
    return false unless File.exist?(file_path)

    # Use identify from ImageMagick to validate the image
    identify_path = find_identify_binary
    return false unless identify_path

    system(identify_path, "-regard-warnings", file_path, out: File::NULL, err: File::NULL)
  end

  def find_convert_binary
    # Look for ImageMagick 7's magick binary first, then fall back to convert
    %w[/usr/bin/magick /usr/local/bin/magick /usr/bin/convert /usr/local/bin/convert].each do |path|
      return path if File.executable?(path)
    end

    # Try which as a fallback using array form of system
    path = `which magick 2>/dev/null`.strip
    return path if path.present? && File.executable?(path)

    path = `which convert 2>/dev/null`.strip
    return path if path.present? && File.executable?(path)

    nil
  end

  def find_identify_binary
    %w[/usr/bin/identify /usr/local/bin/identify].each do |path|
      return path if File.executable?(path)
    end

    path = `which identify 2>/dev/null`.strip
    return path if path.present? && File.executable?(path)

    nil
  end
end
